compiled_state.title: git-fixtures.sh: _DSO_GC_AUTO_ZERO export may leak between parallel test processes
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Source: code review (passed) — minor finding not addressed in this session.
Finding: _DSO_GC_AUTO_ZERO is exported as a process-level env var from clone_ticket_repo() in git-fixtures.sh. This may affect tests that source git-fixtures.sh in parallel processes or nested test scenarios where gc.auto is not pre-set to 0.
File: tests/lib/git-fixtures.sh
Category: maintainability

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). The export statement is intentional code that has an unintended side effect in parallel/sequential test execution. Severity=low (0), Complexity=simple/single-file (0), Environment=local (0), Intermittent=deterministic (0). But +1 for the leakage behavior → Score: 1 → BASIC tier.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned — the bug clearly describes an unintended side effect (env var leakage), not a feature request. The export was added for a perf optimization but the intent was never to allow cross-test contamination.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fix applied: removed 'export' from _DSO_GC_AUTO_ZERO in clone_ticket_repo(). Changed to plain assignment so var is visible to sourced libs (ticket-lib.sh) but not exported to child processes. Added RED test test_clone_ticket_repo_does_not_export_gc_auto_zero. Commit: fdfcbb2fd5.
comments.author: Test
